name: Monojit Goswami
about_me: Self-taught Backend Developer specializing in RAG-based systems and high-performance Machine Learning pipelines. backend developer driven by the potential of autonomous systems. My architecture philosophy focuses on efficiency, modularity, security, and scalability, building high-performance engines that bridge the gap between architectural stability and artificial intelligence. I specialize in developing general-purpose, high-fidelity backend solutions and RAG-based agentic systems. Also with interest in DSA. I love to build projects to solve my own solutions and help others, love to guide and mentor others in the same field requiring guidance
tools: OS: Arch Linux | Kernel: Linux Stable | DE: Hyprland (end4 dots) | Terminal: Kitty | Shell: Fish | Tools: VS Code, Vim | Hardware: Dell G15 5525

projects.description: A production-grade RAG-based SaaS chatbot designed for educational institutions to provide 24x7 student support. It features a real-time admin panel for knowledge base management, dynamic system instruction control, and an analytics dashboard to track unresolved queries. To ensure reliability, it implements a confidence-threshold-based human handoff mechanism to prevent hallucinations in high-stakes academic contexts.

A multi-tenant SaaS evolution of Shiksha Saathi that provides isolated, institution-specific RAG chatbot instances. It ensures strict tenant isolation with dedicated vector stores and configurations for each organization. The platform includes a secure, role-based admin system and centralized infrastructure management to support scaling across multiple institutions without compromising data security.

An LLM-powered static code analysis tool designed to infer time and space complexity from source code. It utilizes language-aware understanding to evaluate algorithmic patterns and provide targeted optimization suggestions. Primarily built for educational and screening use cases, it helps developers understand performance trade-offs through actionable feedback and complexity estimation.

A terminal-style portfolio chatbot featuring a controlled roast persona layered over factual information about my technical experience. It operates on a strictly prompt-scoped context to prevent hallucinations and uses Redis for global and per-user rate limiting. The system maintains multi-turn conversation history within a bounded context window to ensure a consistent and engaging user experience.

A privacy-focused, client-side chatbot builder that allows users to prototype LLM chatbots using their own API keys without a backend. It supports multiple providers like Gemini and Groq, offering a secure playground to test system prompts and streaming toggles. Users can export production-ready starter templates for Python or Node.js, including environment files and test scripts for immediate deployment.

A high-performance mass certificate generation tool that operates entirely on the client side using Web Workers for parallelized processing. It features a visual template editor with CSV field mapping and advanced typography controls via Google Fonts. By splitting tasks into large-scale batches, it optimizes memory usage and achieves significant speedups while ensuring user data privacy.
